description_long: This object is a vintage CRT computer monitor with a square, slightly recessed display framed by a thick, beige bezel. The casing is made of molded plastic with a matte finish, featuring ventilation grilles on both sides and the rear for heat dissipation. The back panel includes a warning label, a power socket, and additional ventilation slots, all molded into the plastic shell. The monitor sits on a wide, flat pedestal base that provides stability, and the overall design is boxy with sharp, angular lines typical of late 20th-century electronics. No visible buttons or controls are present on the front or sides, and the screen area is dark, indicating it is powered off. The color is a uniform light beige, with no visible branding or decorative elements.
description: A vintage beige CRT computer monitor with a thick bezel, boxy plastic casing, rear ventilation, and a wide flat base.
description_view_0: Front view shows the square, dark display framed by a thick beige bezel and a wide pedestal base.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the deep, angular casing and side ventilation grille.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals extensive ventilation slots, a warning label, and a power socket at the bottom.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the side ventilation grille and the monitor's depth.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, flat, and made of matte beige plastic with sharp corners and no decorative elements or color contrast.
attribute_stand_type: The stand is a wide, flat pedestal base made of the same beige plastic as the casing, with no visible adjustability features.
attribute_button_placement: No physical buttons or controls are visible on the front, sides, or rear of the monitor.
attribute_port_visibility: A single power socket is visible on the lower rear panel; no other ports are visible.
